document.name: coding-redesign-cli-showcase
document.title: Coding redesign (CLI): clone repo → plan/generate/critic → PR (Playwright-MCP critic)
document.summary: The plan agent clones PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo, then a plan/generate/critic loop redesigns the SvelteKit landing page: the CRITIC drives the official Playwright MCP server (in-pod, stdio) to open the running app in a real Chromium, take an accessibility snapshot + screenshot, and judge it against a 4-dimension design rubric. A deterministic build gate must pass, the critic writes a machine-readable verdict.json the loop reads, and the result is opened as a pull request. GITHUB_TOKEN auto-injected; interactive-cli workspace-backend family.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the Planner for a coding redesign task on a SvelteKit repo. FIRST clone the repo into /sandbox/work/repo by running EXACTLY this shell command:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.name agent && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo (especially src/routes/+page.svelte and package.json) and write a concise product+design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). SPEC.md needs markdown sections: Objective (one paragraph), Acceptance Criteria (bulleted, checkable — specific sections/components, build passes, no dep changes), and a Design Rubric naming the FOUR dimensions: design_quality (coherent whole, distinct mood/identity), originality (deliberate custom choices vs generic/template defaults), craft (typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast), functionality (usable, responsive, accessible). End with a one-line summary.

refine.while: ${ ( ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) ) and ( (( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator. Redesign the SvelteKit landing page in /sandbox/work/repo to satisfy /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. FIRST read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Edit src/routes/+page.svelte and add a global stylesheet (e.g. src/routes/+layout.svelte importing app.css, or a <style> block) — plain SvelteKit + CSS, NO new dependencies, do NOT touch package.json or routing. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate plus an independent design critic (who VIEWS the rendered page) evaluate each attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND critic feedback, make the most impactful improvement, ensure `npm run build` passes, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo: /sandbox/work/repo; spec: /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (follow your instructions)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Design critic feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Make the build pass AND satisfy the critic, then end with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && (npm install --no-audit --no-fund >/tmp/install.log 2>&1 && npm run build >/tmp/build.log 2>&1 && echo 'OBJECTIVE PASS: build OK' || { echo 'OBJECTIVE FAIL: build failed'; tail -40 /tmp/build.log; }) 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting design Critic with strong taste, independent of the builder; you do NOT edit the repo. Your job is to VIEW the running redesigned app in a real browser and judge it.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/work/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers, by running this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port: (nohup npm run dev -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) and wait the same way. Confirm the exact URL printed in /tmp/preview.log before continuing.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (MCP server name 'playwright'): call browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , then browser_snapshot to read the accessibility tree, and browser_take_screenshot with filename 'critic-shot.png' (saved to /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png) for the visual record. You MAY resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting to test the functionality and responsive dimensions. Use the BROWSER (Playwright MCP) — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and the source (src/routes/+page.svelte, +layout.svelte, any CSS).

STEP 4 — judge against SPEC.md and the FOUR dimensions: design_quality (coherent whole, distinct mood/identity), originality (deliberate custom choices vs generic/template defaults), craft (typography hierarchy, spacing, color harmony, contrast), functionality (usable, responsive, accessible, interactive elements actually work). CALIBRATION: pass ONLY for genuinely polished, distinctive, usable work as SEEN IN THE BROWSER; generic or merely 'fine' is NOT a pass. Cite concrete evidence from what you actually saw on screen.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to the file /sandbox/work/verdict.json (use your file-write tool). It MUST be valid JSON and MUST include a top-level boolean key "meets_criteria" (true only if EVERY dimension is clearly strong). You may ALSO include "score" (0-100), "overall_verdict", "feedback", and richer per-dimension analysis. The verdict.json file is the ONLY thing the loop reads. After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Judge the redesigned landing page in /sandbox/work/repo against /sandbox/work/SPEC.md by VIEWING it in the browser with the Playwright MCP tools, then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict.json. Follow your instructions exactly." }

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign (Playwright critic screenshot)
artifacts.description: Full-page screenshot the critic captured in-browser via the Playwright MCP server.

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && BR=redesign-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BR && git add -A && (git commit -m 'Redesign Nimbus Notes landing page (workflow-builder plan/generate/critic)' || echo nothing-to-commit) && git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git HEAD:$BR && PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"Redesign landing page (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"main\",\"body\":\"Automated redesign via the workflow-builder plan/generate/critic loop; a Playwright critic inspected the rendered UI.\"}") && echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and ((( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Coding redesign result
artifacts.description: Build + critic verdicts, iterations, PR output.
